content: Orchestrator, per approve-initiative.md's own instruction: before approving, confirm which domains are affected by fe7be6ff's changes and whether Owns/Does-Not-Own boundaries are respected.

orchestrator-role/SKILL.md and trail-schema.md are Platform engine files (catalog/Platform/PMCR-O/), not owned by any single C-Suite domain -- every domain's cycles route through pmcro-loop, so a change here affects all 10 domains uniformly. Confirm this is within scope for a ceo-dispatched approval rather than requiring each domain's individual sign-off, per ceo:ceo's own scope: 'approval of major cross-agent actions.'

Re-read fe7be6ff's 01-check.jsonl and disposition.json in full before ratifying -- confirm handoff_verified: true and disposition: needs-approval (not reject or needs-revision) still hold as written, rather than trusting the prior turn's summary.

If approved: update .clinerules header from 'staged, needs-approval... Not yet ratified' to ratified status, referencing this trail as the approval record. Do not edit fe7be6ff's own sealed files (Article 3) -- the ratification lives in this new trail plus a status-line edit to .clinerules itself, which is a live repo file, not a sealed trail artifact.

Flag for checker: unlike fe7be6ff, this cycle's Make step does not touch catalog/skills.json, a marketplace.json twin, or another domain's SKILL.md -- only root .clinerules and this trail's own files. Per hil-gating.md that keeps the write itself TYPE2; the eventual git commit/push is TYPE1 regardless and still needs a HIL checkpoint before execution, separate from whether this trail can self-seal accept.
